threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of 'the Overground' when you're waiting at Dalston Junction. That'll change fast, trust me.
Last night, a guy on the 69 was serenading the driver with an old Adele song. Only in Brixton would that be perfectly normal at 2 AM.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a guy serenading the rain outside Reuterplatz with an old guitar while the buses went by. Felt like I belonged.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Friedrichshain, you'll find random street art pop-up galleries in abandoned buildings, showcasing local artists without any official permits. It's a wild mix of creativity.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Locals know to never trust the BART schedule. If they aren’t checking their watch every 5 mins, they’re likely just passing through.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 47 near Union Square this morning. The bus driver stopped mid-route to help a lost tourist. You don’t see that kind of kindness everywhere.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all locals dine late, but many of us prefer a 9 PM dinner to beat the crowds—especially on weekends.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their confusion over how to order a coffee. It's always 'un café amb llet, per favor,' with that endearing hesitation.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of 'the Overground' when you hop on at Dalston Junction. That's an instant giveaway.
Caught a night bus near Brixton last week, and a group of teenagers debated the best chicken spots while a stray cat jumped on the seat next to me.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw kids playing football with a makeshift goal made of empty beer crates on the street, I knew I found my home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how the street art in Kreuzberg shifts with the weather? Rain washes away layers, revealing old tags like ghosts from the past.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they're local if they can order a burrito without flinching at the price, but look genuinely shocked when their BART train's late again.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Watched a guy try to start a dance battle on the 49 while the bus was stuck in traffic. It turned into a mini talent show—classic SF chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia’s just about the Gaudí sites. They miss the tiny squares where locals actually gather for vermut, laughing in the sun.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You’ll catch them double-checking the metro map at 9 PM, still baffled that some stations close early on weekends.
